contents: From HoTT Require Import Basics Types.
From HoTT Require Import Basics Types.
Generalizable Variables X A B f g n.

(* ** Definition of the [Circle]. *)

(** We define the circle as the coequalizer of two copies of the identity map of [Unit].  This is easily equivalent to the naive definition

<<
Private Inductive Circle : Type0 :=
| base : Circle
| loop : base = base.
>>

but it allows us to apply the flattening lemma directly rather than having to pass across that equivalence.  *)

(** The circle is defined to be the coequalizer of two copies of the identity map on [Unit]. *)
Definition Circle := @Coeq Unit Unit idmap idmap.
Definition loop : base = base := cglue tt.
(** Here is a notation for the circle that can be imported. *)

Definition Circle_ind (P : Circle -> Type)
  (b : P base) (l : loop # b = b)
  : forall (x : Circle), P x.

goals.conclusion: forall x : Circle, P x
forall x : Circle, P x

contents: refine (Coeq_ind P (fun u => transport P (ap coeq (path_unit tt u)) b) _).

goals.conclusion:
forall b0 : Unit,
transport P (cglue b0) (transport P (ap coeq (path_unit tt b0)) b) =
transport P (ap coeq (path_unit tt b0)) b

contents:
Definition Circle_ind_beta_loop (P : Circle -> Type)
  (b : P base) (l : loop # b = b)
  : apD (Circle_ind P b l) loop = l
  := Coeq_ind_beta_cglue P _ _ tt.
(** We mark [Circle], [base] and [loop] to never be simplified by [simpl] or [cbn] in order to hide how we defined it from the user. *)
(** The recursion principle or non-dependent eliminator. *)

Definition Circle_rec (P : Type) (b : P) (l : b = b)
  : Circle -> P
  := Circle_ind (fun _ => P) b (transport_const _ _ @ l).

Definition Circle_rec_beta_loop (P : Type) (b : P) (l : b = b)
  : ap (Circle_rec P b l) loop = l.

goals.conclusion: ap (Circle_rec P b l) loop = l
ap (Circle_rec P b l) loop = l
ap (Circle_ind (fun _ : Circle => P) b (transport_const loop b @ l)) loop = l

transport_const loop b @
ap (Circle_ind (fun _ : Circle => P) b (transport_const loop b @ l)) loop =
transport_const loop b @ l

apD (Circle_ind (fun _ : Circle => P) b (transport_const loop b @ l)) loop =
transport_const loop b @ l

contents:
(** ** The loop space of the [Circle] is the Integers [Int]

  This is the encode-decode style proof a la Licata-Shulman. *)
(** We assume univalence throughout this section. *)

(** First we define the type of codes, this is a type family over the circle. This can be thought of as the covering space by the homotopical real numbers. It is defined by mapping loop to the path given by univalence applied to the automorphism of the integers. We will show that the section of this family at [base] is equivalent to the loop space of the circle. Giving us an equivalence [base = base <~> Int]. *)

Definition Circle_code : Circle -> Type
    := Circle_rec Type Int (path_universe int_succ).
(** Transporting along [loop] gives us the successor automorphism on [Int]. *)

Definition transport_Circle_code_loop (z : Int)
    : transport Circle_code loop z = int_succ z.

goals.conclusion: transport Circle_code loop z = z.+1%int
transport Circle_code loop z = z.+1%int
transport idmap (ap Circle_code loop) z = z.+1%int
transport idmap (path_universe int_succ) z = z.+1%int

contents: (** Transporting along [loop^] gives us the predecessor on [Int]. *)

Definition transport_Circle_code_loopV (z : Int)
    : transport Circle_code loop^ z = int_pred z.

goals.conclusion: transport Circle_code loop^ z = z.-1%int
transport Circle_code loop^ z = z.-1%int
transport idmap (ap Circle_code loop^) z = z.-1%int
transport idmap (ap Circle_code loop)^ z = z.-1%int
transport idmap (path_universe int_succ)^ z = z.-1%int
transport idmap (path_universe int_succ^-1) z = z.-1%int

contents: (** To turn a path in [Circle] based at [base] into a code we transport along it. We call this encoding. *)

Definition Circle_encode (x:Circle) : (base = x) -> Circle_code x
    := fun p => p # zero.

(** TODO: explain this proof in more detail. *)
  (** Turning a code into a path based at [base]. We call this decoding. *)
Definition Circle_decode (x : Circle) : Circle_code x -> (base = x).

goals.conclusion: Circle_code x -> base = x
Circle_code x -> base = x

contents: revert x; refine (Circle_ind (fun x => Circle_code x -> base = x) (loopexp loop) _).

goals.conclusion:
transport (fun x : Circle => Circle_code x -> base = x) loop (loopexp loop) =
loopexp loop

contents: apply path_forall; intros z; simpl in z.

goals.conclusion:
transport (fun x : Circle => Circle_code x -> base = x) loop (loopexp loop) z =
loopexp loop z

transport (paths base) loop (loopexp loop (transport Circle_code loop^ z)) =
loopexp loop z
loopexp loop (transport Circle_code loop^ z) @ loop = loopexp loop z
loopexp loop z.-1%int @ loop = loopexp loop z
(loopexp loop z @ loop^) @ loop = loopexp loop z

contents: (** The non-trivial part of the proof that decode and encode are equivalences is showing that decoding followed by encoding is the identity on the fibers over [base]. *)

Definition Circle_encode_loopexp (z : Int)
    : Circle_encode base (loopexp loop z) = z.

goals.conclusion: Circle_encode base (loopexp loop z) = z
Circle_encode base (loopexp loop z) = z
Circle_encode base (loopexp loop 0%int) = 0%int
Circle_encode base (loopexp loop n.+1%int) = n.+1%int
Circle_encode base (loopexp loop (- n).-1%int) = (- n).-1%int
Circle_encode base (loopexp loop 0%int) = 0%int
Circle_encode base (loopexp loop n.+1%int) = n.+1%int
Circle_encode base (loopexp loop n @ loop) = n.+1%int
transport Circle_code (loopexp loop n @ loop) 0%int = n.+1%int

transport Circle_code loop (transport Circle_code (loopexp loop n) 0%int) =
n.+1%int
transport Circle_code loop n = n.+1%int
Circle_encode base (loopexp loop (- n).-1%int) = (- n).-1%int
Circle_encode base (loopexp loop (- n)%int @ loop^) = (- n).-1%int
transport Circle_code (loopexp loop (- n)%int @ loop^) 0%int = (- n).-1%int

transport Circle_code loop^
  (transport Circle_code (loopexp loop (- n)%int) 0%int) =
(- n).-1%int
transport Circle_code loop^ (- n)%int = (- n).-1%int
IsEquiv (Circle_encode x)
IsEquiv (Circle_encode x)
(fun x0 : Circle_code x => Circle_encode x (Circle_decode x x0)) == idmap
(fun x0 : base = x => Circle_decode x (Circle_encode x x0)) == idmap

contents: (* Here we induct on [x:Circle].  We just did the case when [x] is [base]. *)

goals.conclusion: (fun x0 : Circle_code x => Circle_encode x (Circle_decode x x0)) == idmap

contents:
refine (Circle_ind (fun x => (Circle_encode x) o (Circle_decode x) == idmap)
        Circle_encode_loopexp _ _).

goals.conclusion:
transport
  (fun x0 : Circle =>
   (fun x1 : Circle_code x0 => Circle_encode x0 (Circle_decode x0 x1)) ==
   idmap)
  loop Circle_encode_loopexp =
Circle_encode_loopexp

contents: (* What remains is easy since [Int] is known to be a set. *)
by apply path_forall; intros z; apply hset_path2.
(* The other side is trivial by path induction. *)

goals.conclusion: (fun x0 : base = x => Circle_decode x (Circle_encode x x0)) == idmap

contents: (** Finally giving us an equivalence between the loop space of the [Circle] and [Int]. *)

Definition equiv_loopCircle_int : (base = base) <~> Int
    := Build_Equiv _ _ (Circle_encode base) (Circle_encode_isequiv base).

(** ** Connectedness and truncatedness of the [Circle] *)

(** The circle is 0-connected. *)

goals.conclusion: IsConnected (Tr 0) Circle
IsConnected (Tr 0) Circle
merely Circle
forall x y : Circle, merely (x = y)
forall x y : Circle, merely (x = y)
(fun x : Circle => forall y : Circle, merely (x = y)) base
transport (fun x : Circle => forall y : Circle, merely (x = y)) loop ?b = ?b
(fun x : Circle => forall y : Circle, merely (x = y)) base
(fun x : Circle => trunctype_type (merely (base = x))) base
transport (fun x : Circle => merely (base = x)) loop ?b = ?b
(fun x : Circle => trunctype_type (merely (base = x))) base
transport (fun x : Circle => merely (base = x)) loop (tr 1) = tr 1

transport (fun x : Circle => forall y : Circle, merely (x = y)) loop
  (Circle_ind (fun x : Circle => merely (base = x)) 
     (tr 1)
     (path_ishprop
        (transport (fun x : Circle => merely (base = x)) loop (tr 1)) 
        (tr 1))) =
Circle_ind (fun x : Circle => merely (base = x)) (tr 1)
  (path_ishprop (transport (fun x : Circle => merely (base = x)) loop (tr 1))
     (tr 1))

contents: (** It follows that the circle is a 1-type. *)

goals.conclusion: IsTrunc 1 Circle
IsTrunc 1 Circle
forall x y : Circle, IsHSet (x = y)
IsHSet (x = y)
IsHSet (x = y)
IsHSet (x = y)
IsHSet (x = y)
IsHSet (base = base)

contents:
(** ** Iteration of equivalences *)

(** If [P : Circle -> Type] is defined by a type [X] and an auto-equivalence [f], then the image of [n : Int] regarded as in [base = base] is [iter_int f n]. *)

Definition Circle_action_is_iter `{Univalence} X (f : X <~> X) (n : Int) (x : X)
: transport (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) (equiv_loopCircle_int^-1 n) x
  = int_iter f n x.

goals.conclusion:
transport (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) (equiv_loopCircle_int^-1 n) x =
int_iter f n x

transport (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) (equiv_loopCircle_int^-1 n) x =
int_iter f n x

transport (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) (equiv_loopCircle_int^-1 n) x =
transport idmap (loopexp (path_universe f) n) x

transport idmap
  (ap (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) (equiv_loopCircle_int^-1 n)) x =
transport idmap (loopexp (path_universe f) n) x

contents: refine (ap (fun p => transport idmap p x) _).

goals.conclusion:
ap (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) (equiv_loopCircle_int^-1 n) =
loopexp (path_universe f) n

ap (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) (Circle_decode base n) =
loopexp (path_universe f) n

ap (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) (loopexp loop n) =
loopexp (path_universe f) n

loopexp (ap (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) loop) n =
loopexp (path_universe f) n
ap (Circle_rec Type X (path_universe f)) loop = path_universe f

contents: (** The universal property of the circle (Lemma 6.2.9 in the Book).  We could deduce this from [isequiv_Coeq_rec], but it's nice to see a direct proof too. *)

Definition Circle_rec_uncurried (P : Type)
  : {b : P & b = b} -> (Circle -> P)
  := fun x => Circle_rec P (pr1 x) (pr2 x).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (Circle_rec_uncurried P)
IsEquiv (Circle_rec_uncurried P)
(Circle -> P) -> {b : P & b = b}
Circle_rec_uncurried P o ?g == idmap
?g o Circle_rec_uncurried P == idmap
(Circle -> P) -> {b : P & b = b}

contents: exact (fun g => (g base ; ap g loop)).

goals.conclusion:
Circle_rec_uncurried P o (fun g : Circle -> P => (g base; ap g loop)) ==
idmap
Circle_rec_uncurried P (g base; ap g loop) = g
Circle_rec_uncurried P (g base; ap g loop) == g
(fun x : Circle => Circle_rec_uncurried P (g base; ap g loop) x = g x) base

transport
  (fun x : Circle => Circle_rec_uncurried P (g base; ap g loop) x = g x) loop
  ?b =
?b
(fun x : Circle => Circle_rec_uncurried P (g base; ap g loop) x = g x) base

transport
  (fun x : Circle => Circle_rec_uncurried P (g base; ap g loop) x = g x) loop
  1 =
1
ap (Circle_rec_uncurried P (g base; ap g loop)) loop @ 1 = 1 @ ap g loop
ap (Circle_rec_uncurried P (g base; ap g loop)) loop = ap g loop

(fun g : Circle -> P => (g base; ap g loop)) o Circle_rec_uncurried P ==
idmap
ap (Circle_rec_uncurried P (b; p)) loop = p

contents:
Definition equiv_Circle_rec `{Funext} (P : Type)
  : {b : P & b = b} <~> (Circle -> P)
  := Build_Equiv _ _ _ (isequiv_Circle_rec_uncurried P).
(** A pointed version of the universal property of the circle. *)

Definition pmap_from_circle_loops `{Funext} (X : pType)
  : (pCircle ->** X) <~>* loops X.

goals.conclusion: (pCircle ->** X) <~>* loops X
(pCircle ->** X) <~>* loops X
(pCircle ->** X) <~> loops X
?f pt = pt
(pCircle ->** X) <~> loops X
{f : pCircle -> X & f pt = pt} <~> loops X

contents: equiv_via { xp : { x : X & x = x } & xp.1 = pt }.

goals.conclusion: {f : pCircle -> X & f pt = pt} <~> {xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt}
{xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt} <~> loops X
{f : pCircle -> X & f pt = pt} <~> {xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt}

(equiv_composeR' (equiv_functor_sigma_pb (equiv_Circle_rec X)^-1)
   (equiv_adjointify
      (fun H0 : {xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt} =>
       (fun H1 : {x : X & x = x} =>
        (fun (H2 : X) (H3 : H2 = H2) (H4 : (H2; H3).1 = pt) =>
         paths_ind_r pt (fun (y : X) (_ : y = pt) => y = y -> loops X)
           (fun H5 : pt = pt => H5 : loops X) H2 H4 H3)
          H1.1 H1.2)
         H0.1 H0.2)
      (fun H0 : loops X =>
       ((let H1 := pt in H1;
        H0 : (let H1 := pt in H1) = (let H1 := pt in H1))
        :
        {x : X & x = x};
       1%path
       :
       ((let H1 := pt in H1;
        H0 : (let H1 := pt in H1) = (let H1 := pt in H1))
        :
        {x : X & x = x}).1 =
       pt)
       :
       {xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt})
      ((fun H0 : loops X => 1%path)
       :
       (fun H0 : {xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt} =>
        (fun H1 : {x : X & x = x} =>
         (fun (H2 : X) (H3 : H2 = H2) (H4 : (H2; H3).1 = pt) =>
          paths_ind_r pt (fun (y : X) (_ : y = pt) => y = y -> loops X)
            (fun H5 : pt = pt => H5 : loops X) H2 H4 H3)
           H1.1 H1.2)
          H0.1 H0.2)
       o (fun H0 : loops X =>
          ((let H1 := pt in H1;
           H0 : (let H1 := pt in H1) = (let H1 := pt in H1))
           :
           {x : X & x = x};
          1%path
          :
          ((let H1 := pt in H1;
           H0 : (let H1 := pt in H1) = (let H1 := pt in H1))
           :
           {x : X & x = x}).1 =
          pt)
          :
          {xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt}) ==
       idmap)
      ((fun H0 : {xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt} =>
        (fun H1 : {x : X & x = x} =>
         (fun (H2 : X) (H3 : H2 = H2) (H4 : (H2; H3).1 = pt) =>
          paths_ind_r pt
            (fun (y : X) (p : y = pt) =>
             forall H5 : y = y,
             ((let H6 := pt in H6;
              paths_ind_r pt
                (fun (y0 : X) (_ : y0 = pt) => y0 = y0 -> loops X) idmap y p
                H5);
             1%path) = ((y; H5); p))
            (fun H5 : pt = pt => 1%path) H2 H4 H3)
           H1.1 H1.2)
          H0.1 H0.2)
       :
       (fun H0 : loops X =>
        ((let H1 := pt in H1;
         H0 : (let H1 := pt in H1) = (let H1 := pt in H1))
         :
         {x : X & x = x};
        1%path
        :
        ((let H1 := pt in H1;
         H0 : (let H1 := pt in H1) = (let H1 := pt in H1))
         :
         {x : X & x = x}).1 =
        pt)
        :
        {xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt})
       o (fun H0 : {xp : {x : X & x = x} & xp.1 = pt} =>
          (fun H1 : {x : X & x = x} =>
           (fun (H2 : X) (H3 : H2 = H2) (H4 : (H2; H3).1 = pt) =>
            paths_ind_r pt (fun (y : X) (_ : y = pt) => y = y -> loops X)
              (fun H5 : pt = pt => H5 : loops X) H2 H4 H3)
             H1.1 H1.2)
            H0.1 H0.2) ==
       idmap))
 oE (issig_pmap pCircle X)^-1) pt =
pt
ap (fun _ : Circle => pt) loop = pt
